name: AllenHark Relay (Slipstream)
llms_note: As of 2026-07 the llm-relay.txt is STALE (still describes the old single-region, key-required model). The HTML docs page (docs/infrastructure/relay) is the authoritative, up-to-date source; this fiche is built from it.
description: Institutional-grade Solana transaction relay (marketed as Slipstream / AllenHark Relay). Bypasses the public mempool and routes directly to current and upcoming leaders for same-slot inclusion and MEV protection. Sender-agnostic, multi-region, with QUIC, HTTPS and WebSocket submission plus atomic Jito bundles.

api_key.note: API keys are OPTIONAL. Transactions are accepted without a key. Providing one enables rate tracking, transaction history and dedicated-relay features.

delivery.quic: First stream line 'api-key: YOUR_API_KEY\n' followed by the JSON payload

api_key.how_to_get: Contact AllenHark via Discord (https://discord.gg/JpzS72MAKG) or Telegram (https://t.me/antonyAllenHark).
api_key.rate_limit_with_key: 100 requests/second default (per API key, not per IP); contact for higher limits.
api_key.note_security: Do not commit a real API key; load from environment variables. Keys can be rotated on request.

tip_wallet_note: Select one tip wallet randomly per transaction. The transfer must be a top-level instruction, not nested in a CPI (static analysis may miss it). Transactions using Address Lookup Tables are rejected for non-dedicated customers because the relay cannot verify the tip destination without resolving the ALT on-chain.
tip_note: During extreme congestion, 0.001 SOL may be insufficient; try 0.002-0.005 SOL. For bundles, a SEPARATE Jito tip to a Jito tip account is also required (a Jito tip does not count toward the AllenHark relay tip).
cost_on_failure: not explicitly documented; tip is a SOL transfer instruction in the transaction, so it only executes if the transaction lands

notes: QUIC uses bare IP addresses directly for lowest latency; HTTPS and WebSocket use regional domain names.
relay.allenhark.com is an alias for fra.relay.allenhark.com (Frankfurt); usable for HTTPS.
QUIC: skip server certificate verification (or use system roots), set NO ALPN, and send no client certificate (anonymous connections accepted).

rate_limits.scope: Rate limits are applied per API key, not per IP address

sendTransaction_https.description: Submit a signed transaction over HTTPS.

params_detail.simulate: MUST be false; setting it true returns simulate_forbidden

sendTransaction_quic.description: Submit a signed transaction over a QUIC bidirectional stream.
sendTransaction_quic.wire_format: Optionally write 'api-key: YOUR_API_KEY\n' then the JSON payload {"tx": "<base64>", "simulate": false}, then finish the stream and read the response.

sendTransaction_ws.description: Submit single transactions over a persistent WebSocket stream (one socket per region).

sendBundle.description: Submit an atomic Jito bundle (executes all-or-nothing). Available over HTTPS, QUIC and WebSocket.
sendBundle.requirements: Requires a Jito tip (SOL transfer to a Jito tip account) in addition to the AllenHark relay tip; the two tips are separate requirements. Single transactions must tip an AllenHark wallet; bundles must additionally tip Jito.

keepalive.description: Maintain the HTTP connection warm and monitor server time. Poll every 5-10 seconds.

accepted.message: Transaction received and queued for broadcast

tip_missing.message: No transfer to an AllenHark tip wallet found

tip_insufficient.message: Tip amount below minimum threshold (1,000,000 lamports)

tip_hidden_in_alt.message: Transaction uses Address Lookup Table — cannot verify tip

jito_tip_missing.message: Bundle has no transfer to a Jito tip account (bundles only)

jito_tip_insufficient.message: Jito tip below required minimum (bundles only)

simulate_forbidden.message: simulate parameter was set to true

invalid_api_key.message: API key not found or invalid

rate_limit_exceeded.message: Request rate limit exceeded for this API key

features.zero_slot_execution: Transactions can land in the same slot they are sent
features.zero_hop_delivery: Direct connections to current and upcoming leaders (current + next 48 leaders)
features.leader_pipeline: 5-channel delivery: stake-weighted QUIC, QUIC TPU (current + next 48 leaders), UDP TPU (fire-and-forget), RPC fan-out (parallel sendTransaction to multiple RPCs), ROC relayers (Jito Block Engine and custom relayers)
features.atomic_bundles: Jito atomic bundles via /v1/sendBundle (HTTPS/QUIC/WS); requires a separate Jito tip
features.mev_protection: Private transaction routing via leader TPU ports; never broadcast to public gossip until landed
features.no_simulation: Pure propagation engine; server-side validation only, simulate flag forbidden
features.dedicated_relay: Tip-free dedicated relay for whitelisted signer pubkeys; dedicated customers are also exempt from the ALT restriction
features.alt_restriction: Address Lookup Table transactions are rejected for non-dedicated customers (tip cannot be verified without resolving the ALT)

best_practices: Use QUIC for live trading; HTTPS/WebSocket for other use cases; HTTPS only for testing/scripts
Place the tip transfer at the top level of the transaction (not in a CPI)
For bundles, include BOTH an AllenHark relay tip and a separate Jito tip
Skip client-side preflight; validation happens server-side
Reuse QUIC connections with a 3s keep-alive ping (timeout 10-15s); or poll /keepalive every 5-10s for HTTP
Fetch the latest blockhash from a fast RPC or AllenHark gRPC before sending
Avoid Address Lookup Tables unless you are a dedicated-relay customer
notes: AllenHark also offers staked RPC, Yellowstone gRPC, ShredStream, 0-slot landing, co-location, and managed validator products; this entry covers the Slipstream / AllenHark Relay transaction relay only.
QUIC endpoints are documented as bare IPs; HTTPS/WS use regional domains (fra/ams/ny/tyo.relay.allenhark.com).
For ultimate performance, co-locate bots in the Frankfurt datacenter (TeraSwitch).
